scope_note: QA Harmonic Aliquot Structure cert [322]. Primary source: Iverson, B. (1995) Quantum Arithmetic Book 3 (QA-3), ITAM Portland, ISBN 1-883401-08-9, Chapter 4: 'HARMONICS'. Theory of Harmony (p.52): 'Harmonics, or harmony occurs between two dissimilar cycles of energy when both can be divided into similar aliquot parts having the same magnitude but different multitudes.' Formal QA translation: two waves W1=A*p1 and W2=A*p2 are harmonic iff A=gcd(W1,W2), p1=W1/A and p2=W2/A are distinct primes, and gcd(A,p1*p2)=1. In the mod-9 Cosmos, d-value d=b+e is the semi-major diameter (DO=d^2 from cert [320]); the 15 distinct Cosmos d-values {3,...,17} are the harmonic domain. Five structural results certified: (C1) 20 harmonic dyads among Cosmos d-values; all aliquot parts 7-smooth. (C2) Direction law: for every dyad with d1<d2, p2>p1 — higher d (shorter wavelength, higher harmonic) carries the larger unique prime, confirming Ben's reversal 'The higher harmonics CREATE the lower tone' (p.43). (C3) Aliquot spectrum is exactly {1,2,3,5} — no aliquot > 5 in mod-9 Cosmos; aliquot parts live in the {2,3,5,7} smooth core. (C4) 5040=2^4*3^2*5*7 has omega=4 distinct prime factors; all Cosmos d-values <= 17 < 5040 confirming definite quantum configuration (Ben's Quantum Flexibility threshold p.53). (C5) Exactly 3 Cosmos d-values are primes > 7: {11,13,17}; each forms 5 harmonic dyads (3 with prime d-values {3,5,7} + 2 cross-tonal), giving them the richest harmonic connectivity. Builds on cert [320] (Quantize Algorithm, DO=d^2) and cert [321] (Quantize-to-ONE, ratio b/a). Complements cert [318] (Synchronous Harmonics Ceiling, 5040 extremal). DOI: primary text, no external DOI.
